page: Microsoft Azure D64ps_v5 is an ARM64-based virtual machine belonging to the DPSv5 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores powered by an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor operating at 3.0 GHz. The architecture allocates exactly one thread per core, ensuring no hyperthreading resource contention. The system is equipped with 208.0 GB of RAM, translating to 3.25 GB of memory per core. Cache specifications include 64 KB of L1 data cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache per core, and a shared 32 MB L3 cache. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is generally modest, with weak ratings in Geekbench single-core and bzip3 single-threaded decompression. However, the 64 dedicated physical cores deliver strong multi-core performance, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 112,414.59 and strong results in software compilation and ray tracing. Memory performance is highly efficient for small cached blocks, achieving a read-only bandwidth of 4,385.45 GB/sec, while large-block uncached read and write operations remain average. Redis SET operations are exceptionally strong, reaching 80.8 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D64ps_v5 leverages the power-efficient ARM architecture to deliver high physical core density and strong multi-threaded throughput without the premium cost associated with traditional x86 architectures. The primary tradeoff is the lower single-core performance and the lack of advanced vector or matrix extensions, which limits its suitability for heavy single-threaded tasks or complex mathematical simulations. Additionally, the absence of local storage requires external block storage attachment, which may affect overall I/O latency depending on the configured storage tier.

This server type is highly suited for scale-out multi-threaded workloads that benefit from dedicated physical cores. Appropriate use cases include software compilation pipelines, static web serving, Redis caching layers, and multi-threaded database operations. It is also capable of handling moderate language model inference tasks, particularly text generation with small to medium models, though it is not optimized for large-scale deep learning due to the lack of GPU acceleration.
description: The Microsoft Azure D64ps_v5 is an ARM64-based virtual machine from the DPSv5 family, powered by a 64-core Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz with 208.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features dedicated physical cores with one thread per core, eliminating hyperthreading overhead. While single-core benchmarks like Geekbench and bzip3 decompression show weaker performance, the server excels in multi-threaded workloads, demonstrating strong results in stress-ng multi-core tests, software compilation, and Redis SET operations. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but supports Premium Storage. The D64ps_v5 offers a cost-efficient, high-density hardware profile for scale-out workloads, making it highly suitable for multi-threaded database operations, web serving, and CI/CD compilation pipelines.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64ps_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 cores, 208 GB RAM, and strong multi-core performance for web serving and database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64ps_v5 is a 64-core ARM64 virtual machine with 208 GB RAM, optimized for multi-threaded workloads and web serving.
tagline: An ARM64-powered virtual machine delivering 64 dedicated physical cores and balanced memory for highly concurrent, scale-out workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 ARM64 physical cores running at 3.0 GHz
208.0 GB of system memory providing 3.25 GB per core
Strong multi-core performance for software compilation and Redis operations
No local storage or GPU accelerators, but Premium Storage capable
Well-suited for static web serving, databases, and concurrent CI/CD pipelines
